intended_use: QP-Brain® is a medical imaging processing application intended for automatic labeling and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures and white matter hyperintensities (WMH) from a set o
intended_use_full: QP-Brain® is a medical imaging processing application intended for automatic labeling and volumetric quantification of segmentable brain structures and white matter hyperintensities (WMH) from a set of adults and adolescents 18 and older MR images. Volumetric measurements may be compared to reference percentile data. The application is used by clinicians with proper training, as a support tool in assessment of structural MRIs. Patient management decisions should not be based solely on the results of the device.
target_condition: Brain structural assessment; white matter hyperintensities quantification
other_metrics: DICE Score for Gray Matter: 0.983 (0.981–0.986); DICE Score for White Matter: 0.990 (0.988–0.992); DICE Score for Cerebrospinal Fluid: 0.955 (0.944–0.965); DICE Score for Intracranial Volume: 0.994 (0.994–0.995); Relative Volume Difference GM: 2.846% (2.523–3.008%); Relative Volume Difference WM: 1.643% (0.923–1.684%); Relative Volume Difference CSF: 7.495% (3.950–7.718%); Relative Volume Difference ICV: 0.496% (0.298–0.694%); WMH Low DICE: 0.406 (0.360–0.450), Absolute volume error: 0.455 mL (0.317–0.682 mL), F1 score: 0.701 (0.666–0.739); WMH Medium DICE: 0.622 (0.559–0.699), Absolute volume error: 2.084 mL (1.190–3.704 mL); WMH High DICE: 0.748 (0.707–0.790), Absolute volume error: 2.871 mL (1.311–4.763 mL); WMH Very High DICE: 0.806 (0.677–0.923), Absolute volume error: 5.668 mL (2.853–15.831 mL); Pearson correlation coefficient for WMH count: 0.88
